youtube_items.summary: As we market decades since the adoption of the 2030 agenda, we stand at a turning point in human history. With just five years remaining, we've made remarkable progress, but it's not enough. The 2025 SDG report reveals that only 35% of SDG targets are on track or making moderate progress, with nearly half moving too slowly and 18% are regressing below 2015 baseline levels. The last decade proves that transformation is possible. Millions have gained access to essential services. For the first time, more than half of the world's population is now covered by at least one social protection benefit. Maternal and child mortality rates have fallen. More than 100 million children and youth have entered school, with rising completion rates at all levels. More young people, especially girls, are als

raw_items.title: ‘Real impact on the ground’ needed as Indonesia backs UN reform
raw_items.summary:
Indonesia is expecting a “strengthened multilateral system that delivers real impact on the ground,” as one of the key outcomes of the ongoing reform of the United Nations, that’s according to the country’s outgoing Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs for Multilateral Cooperation, Tri Tharyat.

The post ‘Real impact on the ground’ needed as Indonesia backs UN reform appeared first on
